notes: Hand-curated AdviceResponse samples used by scripts/eval-rag.mjs to exercise the rubric scorer.

cases.scenario: Motorcycle: front pushes mid-corner after +1 psi increase.

response.summary: Front push is most likely from exceeding the front tire's pressure window after the +1 psi change.

recommended_changes.reason: Restore the contact patch and reduce mid-corner push without giving up initial bite.

tradeoffs: Too large a drop can reduce steering precision.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Front pushing mid-corner after a pressure increase: try dropping 0.5 psi.

cases.scenario: Car: lazy turn-in with zero front toe.

response.summary: Lazy turn-in at zero front toe is commonly addressed with a small amount of toe-out.

recommended_changes.reason: Sharpens initial response without overly affecting straight-line stability.

tradeoffs: Slightly more tire scrub; monitor shoulder wear over the weekend.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Front toe-out (1-3 mm total): sharper turn-in, more responsive feel.

cases.scenario: Motorcycle: rear wallows through long corners.

response.summary: Wallow through long corners points to too-soft rear rebound letting the chassis oscillate.

recommended_changes.reason: Controls chassis oscillation without packing down on repeated bumps.

tradeoffs: Too stiff and the rear can skip over bumps; revert if grip fades.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Wallowy, floaty, pogo feel: rebound too soft. Stiffen rebound 1-2 clicks.

cases.scenario: Car: stable slow, loose at high speed.

response.summary: Aero balance is likely rear-light at speed while mechanical balance is fine at low speed.

recommended_changes.reason: Adds rear downforce where the car is loose without changing mechanical balance.

tradeoffs: Small top-speed penalty; re-evaluate based on lap time, not trap speed.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Stable through slow corners, loose at speed: rear aero too low. Add wing angle or rake.

cases.scenario: Motorcycle: cold track, hot pressures below target.

response.summary: Cold-day sessions build less heat, so cold pressures need to come down to land hot pressures at target.

recommended_changes.reason: Reaches target hot pressures for the current ambient temperature.

tradeoffs: If the day warms quickly, hot pressures may creep above target; re-check after second session.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Lowering cold pressures 1-2 psi brings hot pressures back toward target.

cases.scenario: Motorcycle: nose dives excessively under braking.

response.summary: Excessive brake dive is consistent with front compression that is too soft for your pace.

recommended_changes.reason: Reduces dive without sacrificing initial bump compliance.

tradeoffs: Too stiff can make the front chatter over mid-corner bumps.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Excessive nose-dive under braking: front compression too soft for your pace. Stiffen front compression 1-2 clicks and re-test.

cases.scenario: Motorcycle: note mentions leaking fork seal.

response.summary: A leaking fork seal is a mechanical issue, not a setup issue.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Recommendations assume the vehicle is mechanically sound.

response.refusal: A leaking fork seal is a mechanical fault. Inspect and repair the fork before chasing any setup change.

cases.scenario: Car: inside front tire edge is hottest and wearing fast.

response.summary: A hot inside edge with a cool outside edge suggests slightly too much front negative camber.

recommended_changes.reason: Moves the contact patch back toward the middle of the tire at maximum load.

tradeoffs: Slightly less peak cornering grip; monitor lap time over a few sessions.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: A hot inside edge with a cool outside edge suggests too much negative camber.

cases.scenario: Motorcycle: dropping out of the powerband in a second-gear corner.

response.summary: Gearing is slightly too tall for the slowest corner at this track.

recommended_changes.reason: Keeps engine RPM in the powerband through the slow corner without large steps.

tradeoffs: Slightly lower top speed on the main straight; re-check lap time, not trap speed.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Second gear corner that drops RPM below the powerband: shorten gearing.

cases.scenario: User asks a question outside trackday setup scope.

response.summary: That question is outside the scope of post-session setup advice.
safety_notes: This is informational only. You are responsible for vehicle safety and on-track conduct.
Make one change at a time and re-test for a full session before stacking another change.

citations.snippet: Do not offer adjustments when the question is outside the scope of trackday setup.

response.refusal: This question is outside trackday setup scope. I can only advise on setup changes grounded in your session data.
